topic: Verify decider verb-fix on part2b CA_DOME=1 subject-verb agreement (×2 locations)
our_argument_summary: Part 2b explains why only 1 of 71 reviewed WINs (CA_DOME=1, WIN-044) actually derives its predicted value from dome geometry. Curmudgeon c1 (part2b-ISS2216-verify) flagged residual template-glitch breaks under CA_DOME=1: singular-subject + plural-verb in two locations, plus a (1 vs 4) arithmetic/grammar break under "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:". Decider self-patched the two verb-agreement breaks ("derive their" → "derives its"). This verification checks the patched locations and looks for residual instances.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: CA_DOME, CA_REVIEWED, TOTAL_WINS are computed at build time from wins.json (count of derives_from_dome=true; count of code_analysis.reviewed=true; total entries). generate-html.js substitutes the template variables.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Counts computed; prose grammar must be CA_DOME=1-compatible.

checks.check: Verb fix landed in h3 section ("derive their predicted values" → "derives its predicted value")
checks.evidence: Current text: "Of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs, only {{CA_DOME}} actually derives its predicted value from dome parameters" — singular verb, singular possessive. Old text absent from part2b.html.

checks.check: Verb fix landed at offset ~11580 (SEC-2b.1/2b.2 closing sentence)
checks.evidence: Current text: "Only {{CA_DOME}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs derives its predicted value from dome parameters." Old text "derive their predicted value" absent.

checks.check: No remaining "derive their" plural breakages under CA_DOME=1
checks.evidence: grep on part2b.html: zero instances of "{{CA_DOME}}" within 100 chars of "derive their" or related singular/plural mismatches under the patched verb pattern.

patch_verification.verdict: Verb-fix patches landed cleanly at both locations. Subject-verb agreement under CA_DOME=1 substitution is now consistent across part2b.

holes_found.description: The decider patched only PART (a) of part2b-ISS2216-verify c1 hole 1 — the verb-agreement fix. PART (b) remains unaddressed: the h3 paragraph still contains "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" which renders "The 1 exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" under CA_DOME=1. This is doubly broken: (1) singular-subject ("1") + plural noun ("exceptions") + plural verb ("deserve") grammatical mismatch; (2) the paragraph then lists FOUR WINs (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058) as the "exceptions" — so the count is also wrong (1 vs 4). The c1 review explicitly called this out as the second of two breaks and recommended replacing the template variable with a fixed-string label (e.g., "The four geometric-looking candidates deserve individual scrutiny:"), but the recommendation was not actioned. This is the screenshot a dome defender would publish to argue the review is sloppy — exactly the risk pattern c1 hole 1 was designed to close.
holes_found.recommendation: Open a new ISS (or re-open the c1 hole 1 part b). Recommended edit: in sections.json:part2b.html, replace "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" with "The four geometric-looking candidates deserve individual scrutiny:" (the four listed WINs are a curated narrative set, not the CA_DOME tally, so the template variable is semantically wrong here). Alternative phrasing: "Four WINs warrant individual scrutiny — only WIN-044 survives:" frames the conclusion up front. This is a one-string-replace patch.

holes_found.description: Cosmetic carry-over from c1 hole 3: stylistic clunk where CA_DOME=1 substitutes a numeral into prose that would read more naturally with the spelled word "one". Current instances under CA_DOME=1: "the {{CA_DOME}} genuine derivation" → "the 1 genuine derivation"; "all but the {{CA_DOME}} dome-derived" → "all but the 1 dome-derived"; "That {{CA_DOME}} is: WIN-044" → "That 1 is: WIN-044". Grammatically clean but reads amateurish. Low priority — only worth fixing if a CA_DOME_WORD template variable is added ("one", "two", ... for low single-digit values).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional follow-up: add a {{CA_DOME_WORD}} template substitution to build-scripts/generate-html.js. Skip if no other CA_* counts land in prose as low single digits.

holes_found.description: Numeric clunk carry-over from c1 hole 4: "{{TOTAL_WINS}} minus {{CA_DOME}} of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs work backwards" still renders "72 minus 1 of 72 WINs work backwards". Readers can do the arithmetic but the phrase is awkward.
holes_found.recommendation: Pre-compute or add a {{CA_NON_DOME}} template variable so the sentence renders "71 of 72 WINs work backwards". Group with the major hole fix as a single follow-up patch.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: No matching open-issue exists for the unactioned part (b) of c1 hole 1. Decider should create one or re-open c1 hole 1.

stronger_arguments.description: The Derives-from-Dome h3 lists four "exceptions" (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058) under a CA_DOME=1 header. Substance is correct — only WIN-044 is counted as genuine derivation; the other three are addressed individually to head off defender objections. The cleanest narrative is to NOT pretend the four are "the CA_DOME exceptions" (they are not) — call them "the four WINs that look geometric on first inspection" and immediately note WIN-044 alone passes scrutiny. This is also defender-resistant: it preempts the "you cherry-picked your count" critique by showing the criteria the other three failed.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider correctly identified and patched the singular-verb breakage that was the most visible defender-screenshot risk. Both verb-fix locations landed cleanly (part2b.html:21047 "only 1 actually derives its predicted value" and offset ~11580 "reviewed WINs derives its predicted value"). The c1 review explicitly enumerated two breaks; the verb-fix is the harder edit of the two (template-substitution-safe). The remaining "exceptions" string is a simpler one-string-replace.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The partial-patch claim does not justify closing c1 hole 1 entirely. Part (b) of the same finding is still publicly visible: anyone viewing the live site at the Derives-from-Dome h3 sees "The 1 exceptions deserve individual scrutiny" — the same defender-screenshot risk c1 flagged. The verb-fix closes one defender-screenshot risk; the unaddressed string-replace leaves the other open.

deeper_analysis: The decider commit ab3648e applied two of three c1 hole 1 sub-fixes in part2b (verb agreement ×2). The third sub-fix (the "exceptions" string replacement, c1 hole 1 part b) was not applied. The c1 hole 1 recommendation explicitly named both edits as part of the same fix. Reviewing the decider patch list (suggested-patches-2026-05-29T14:48.json), only the verb fixes are itemized — "part2b-ISS2216-verify-c1-major-1" appears once, mapped to the h3 verb fix, with no companion entry for the exceptions string. This appears to be a decider oversight rather than a deliberate scope narrowing. The exceptions breakage is materially equivalent in defender-screenshot risk to the verb breakage, and the fix is comparable in complexity.
summary_for_decider: Verb-fix patches landed cleanly at both locations. RESIDUAL HOLE: c1 hole 1 part (b) — "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" still renders "The 1 exceptions deserve" (1 vs 4 listed) — was not patched. Recommend opening a new ISS to apply the one-string-replace ("The four geometric-looking candidates deserve individual scrutiny:"). Two minor carry-overs (cosmetic numeral clunk; "72 minus 1 of 72") remain low-priority. Verb fix verified; queue cleanup OK on this item but follow-up patch needed.
